sourceMovie.title: Star Trek: First Contact
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Star Trek: First Contact works as a science fiction movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The science fiction and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Star Trek: Nemesis
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Star Trek: First Contact clicked for you, Star Trek: Nemesis (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Star Trek: First Contact?
faq.answer: Try Star Trek: Nemesis, Star Trek III: The Search for Spock, Star Trek: Generations—each shares DNA with Star Trek: First Contact while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Star Trek: First Contact?
faq.answer: Star Trek: First Contact (1996) is best described as Science Fiction, Action, Adventure, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Star Trek: First Contact part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Star Trek: The Next Generation Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
